title: Conductor genius merge route platform drain
purpose: B2.1 defines conductor genius merge route platform drain for the agent-driven expert system. Cognition & routing — [S0](B1.1-s0-deterministic-mandatory-first.md)–S4, conductor, workers, dual-stack.
scope: Owns `B2.1` only; siblings under `B2` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).
Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §4 — Branch B — Cognition & routing plane](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#4-branch-b-cognition-routing-plane).
behavior_steps: The genius-tier conductor merges worker summaries each turn, reads route-tier output, and chooses `next_action` without implementing large task cards inline when `spawn_workers` is true.

On the interleaved schedule from Plane D, the conductor may peek or drain one platform promotion queue item per K product steps via [B4.2](B4.2-platform-promotion-queue-peek-drain.md) while product pursuit stays on the main stack.
Only the conductor dual-writes `journal/progress.md` and `journal/state.json` after workers return; workers never clear gates, approve design, or advance `next_action` on their own.
The conductor spawns economy workers with bounded `allowed_reads` from task cards or Librarian output and keeps platform workers confined to queue item ids without mutating consumer goal scope.
If merge would skip S0 preflight, corrupt dual-write, or drain platform work that blocks the active product goal, pursuit stops at H2 until routing and queue policy reconcile.

Edge case `B2.1` variant 3: verify state dual-write before continuing pursuit.
Edge case `B2.1` variant 4: verify state dual-write before continuing pursuit.

narrative: B2.1 defines conductor genius merge route platform drain for the agent-driven expert system. Cognition & routing — [S0](B1.1-s0-deterministic-mandatory-first.md)–S4, conductor, workers, dual-stack. In practice, the system the genius-tier conductor merges worker summaries each turn, reads route-tier output, and chooses `next_action` without implementing large task cards inline when `spawn_workers` is true, then on the interleaved schedule from plane d, the conductor may peek or drain one platform promotion queue item per k product steps via [b4.2](b4.2-platform-promotion-queue-peek-drain.md) while product pursuit stays on the main stack.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent plane-b 2026-06-28 -->

The conductor is genius-tier and thin: merge worker summaries, run S0 preflight, choose next_action, spawn workers, and route platform queue drains—it does not implement large task cards inline when `spawn_workers` is true.

Each turn may peek at or drain the platform promotion queue on the interleaved product-and-improvement schedule so self-improvement work advances alongside the current project. After workers finish, only the conductor dual-writes journal and state.json. This role separation is the core of [genius-conductor-tiered-routing.md](../../genius-conductor-tiered-routing.md).
